reasoning: The informal statement asserts the two-sided limit lim_{x→0} (e^(8x)-1)/x = 8, with no restriction on the sign of x. The Lean statement uses the filter `𝓝[Set.Ioi 0] 0 = nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ioi 0)`, which Mathlib defines as the punctured right-neighborhood filter `𝓝[>] 0`, i.e. it only approaches 0 from x > 0. Thus the Lean statement proves only the right-hand limit equals 8. The two-sided limit equalling 8 implies the right-hand limit equals 8, but not conversely, so the Lean statement is strictly logically weaker than the informal claim. This is not an "implicit premise" case — nothing in standard mathematical reading of "lim_{x→0}" restricts to positive x.
